personality.headline: A Philosopher's Soul Seeking Truth in Silence
personality.description: Born on December 31st, you blend Capricorn's grounded ambition with Life Path 7's deep insight and mystical nature — a 'seeker who doesn't fear solitude.' Born on the last day of the year, you instinctively understand 'endings and beginnings,' viewing life's turning points with calm clarity. You find true happiness not in lively gatherings but in solitary reading or quiet contemplation. Smoothing over surfaces to please others isn't your style; while you may appear cool externally, inside lives a romantic who loves the world more deeply than anyone and is endlessly seeking truth.
personality.theme: Your life theme is 'balancing solitude with connection.' Protect your alone time fiercely, but don't fear building a small circle of deeply trusted bonds. Choose depth over breadth in relationships.

love: In love, you prize 'spiritual connection' above all. You're drawn to inner depth rather than surface chemistry. Love-at-first-sight rarely happens for you; instead, you tend to fall slowly over long stretches of time. Once you open your heart, you offer absolute loyalty — but because you struggle to put feelings into words, partners sometimes misread you as 'cold.' Practicing to verbalize 'thank you' and 'I love you' becomes essential. Your ideal partner is someone intellectual and serene, who experiences your silence as richness rather than loneliness. Marriage tends to feel right from your 30s onward.

career.description: You shine in roles using deep thought and the ability to see essence. Environments demanding instant results drain you; you thrive in jobs allowing careful research, analysis, or creation, especially ones with protected solo focus time.

best.reason: Mutual respect for independence and uniqueness

constitution.description: In traditional astrology, Capricorn governs bones, joints, and teeth. As an earth-element sign, you tend toward 'cold and stagnation' patterns. The introverted energy of Life Path 7 adds mental fatigue and eye strain from heavy thinking — you tend to notice 'overuse' signs late. Being born at year's end aligns you with the body's coldest season.
watchPoints: Coldness and chronic stiffness in knees, lower back, and joints
Eye strain and headaches from overthinking
Reduced sleep quality from a busy mind
constitution.seasonalNote: From December through February, conscious 'warming' and 'releasing' become essential. External warming tools — hot water bottles, belly warmers, moxibustion — help your body soften.

foods.name: Black foods (black beans, black sesame, hijiki)
foods.reason: Traditionally said to support the kidneys and counter cold

foods.name: Walnuts & pine nuts
foods.reason: Nuts said to nourish the brain and nervous system

foods.name: Mountain yam & taro
foods.reason: Sticky-textured roots that support digestive balance

herbs.name: Ginger & cinnamon tea
herbs.reason: Helps warm the body from the core

herbs.name: Chamomile tea
herbs.reason: Said to quiet a busy mind

selfCare.description: Caring for both your 'overthinking head' and 'cold body' is your core practice. Balance among meditation, warming, and digital detox is key.
habits: Begin each day with warm water and quiet breath
Half-body bath twice a week (41°C / 15 min) to warm the pelvis
Evening reading time (paper book + warm lighting)
Monthly nature walks to reconnect with the earth
selfCare.stressManagement: Improving 'input quality' helps more than output. Deep books, quiet music, and time in nature nourish your soul. Frankincense or Cypress aromatherapy is said to support meditation and deep breathing.

famousPeople: Anthony Hopkins (Welsh actor, 'The Silence of the Lambs')
